chapter: Pompey the Great — From Sulla's Lieutenant to Rome's Master (79-62 BCE)
description: Easy Questions — Consulship of 70 BCE, Pirate War, Eastern Commands, Return to Rome

questions.question: What significant constitutional qualification was Pompey missing when he ran for the consulship in 70 BCE?
options: He was too young by two years to hold the office under Sulla's minimum age rules
He had never held any magistracy below the consulship — not the quaestorship, aedileship, or praetorship — and was not technically even a senator
He had not completed the mandatory two-year interval required between his proconsular command in Spain and a new magistracy
He had been born outside Rome and the Sullan constitution restricted the consulship to those born within the city

questions.question: Which of Sulla's constitutional reforms did Pompey and Crassus reverse during their consulship of 70 BCE?
options: They restored the tribunate's full powers, which Sulla had stripped, allowing tribunes to propose legislation directly to the assemblies again
They restored the property qualification for the Senate that Sulla had abolished, reducing its size back to three hundred members
They returned the selection of priests to popular election, reversing Sulla's reinstatement of co-optation by priestly colleges
They abolished the minimum age requirements for magistracies that Sulla had codified in the cursus honorum

questions.question: What was the central problem driving the piracy crisis that prompted the Lex Gabinia in 67 BCE?
options: Pirates had blockaded the port of Ostia, cutting off all seaborne trade with Rome
A pirate confederation had allied with Mithridates of Pontus and was supplying his armies with Roman weapons
Pirates were disrupting grain shipments from Sicily, Sardinia, and Africa, causing grain prices to rise sharply in Rome
Pirates had kidnapped several sitting Roman senators and were holding them for ransom in Cilicia

questions.question: How long did it take Pompey to clear the Mediterranean of pirates after the Lex Gabinia was passed?
options: Three years, the full term granted by the law
Approximately one year, half the allotted time
Three months
Forty days for the western sea, with eastern operations continuing for two more years

questions.question: Rather than executing the tens of thousands of pirates who surrendered, what did Pompey do with them?
options: He sold them into slavery, using the proceeds to fund a new Roman fleet
He enrolled them as auxiliary soldiers in his eastern army, where their maritime skills proved useful
He resettled them inland, reasoning that men driven to piracy by poverty could become productive subjects if given land
He released them on condition they swear an oath never to return to the sea, backed by hostages from each community

questions.question: What caused Lucullus's soldiers to mutiny and refuse to advance further into Armenia?
options: They had not been paid for over a year because Lucullus refused to allow looting of the territories he conquered
They had been in the field for years far from Italy, their legal term of service had long expired, and Lucullus's demanding personality made him respected but unloved
They were frightened of crossing the Euphrates River, which Roman religious tradition held to be the boundary of the civilized world
Lucullus had executed several popular officers for insubordination, turning the entire army against him

questions.question: Which tribune proposed the Lex Manilia in 66 BCE, transferring the eastern command from Lucullus to Pompey?
options: Aulus Gabinius
Publius Sulpicius
Gaius Manilius
Marcus Licinius

questions.question: How did Mithridates eventually die in 63 BCE after his final defeat?
options: He was captured by Pompey's forces and executed at a public ceremony in Rome
He drowned attempting to escape across the Black Sea when his ship was caught in a storm
He attempted suicide by poison but the attempt failed because of his habitual antidote-taking, and he was finally killed by a Gallic soldier
He was betrayed and handed over to Pompey by his son Pharnaces, who then had him strangled

questions.question: What was the first permanent stone theater in Rome, built by Pompey using wealth from his eastern campaigns?
options: The Theatre of Marcellus
The Theatre of Pompey
The Theatre of Balbus
The Theatre of Caesar

questions.question: What surprising decision did Pompey make upon returning to Italy in 62 BCE that stunned contemporaries?
options: He refused to celebrate a triumph, saying the campaign had not been difficult enough to merit one
He disbanded his army rather than marching on Rome as Sulla had done
He immediately announced his retirement from politics and returned to his Campanian estates
He surrendered his eastern command to the Senate without waiting to be recalled

questions.question: What two things did Pompey ask the Senate for after his return from the East, and what did the Senate do?
options: He asked for a triumph and a land grant for himself; the Senate granted the triumph but refused the land grant
He asked for ratification of his eastern settlement and land grants for his veterans; the Senate blocked both
He asked for a second consulship and the title Magnus to be made official; the Senate granted the title but refused the consulship
He asked for command against the Parthians and funding for a new eastern campaign; the Senate refused both on grounds of cost

questions.question: Who were the three men who formed the First Triumvirate in 60 BCE?
options: Pompey, Cicero, and Crassus
Pompey, Caesar, and Lucullus
Pompey, Crassus, and Caesar
Pompey, Cato, and Caesar
